title: The Ambition
author: Alexander Hamilton
content: “A nation which can prefer disgrace to danger is prepared for a master, and deserves one.”

title: The Weight
author: Abraham Lincoln
content: “Nearly all men can stand adversity, but if you want to test a man's character, give him power.”

title: The Constraint
author: James Madison
content: “If men were angels, no government would be necessary.”

title: The Influence
author: Frederick Douglass
content: “Power concedes nothing without a demand. It never did and it never will.”

title: The Legacy
author: Theodore Roosevelt
content: “Far and away the best prize that life has to offer is the chance to work hard at work worth doing.”
